methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
OpenStar, a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Ratu Mataira and Thomas Berry, has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech firms and one public company (LNZA), spanning advanced energy, materials, and fusion technologies at Series A and adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression approach, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement to a $10.0B total-raised proxy, implied a $25.0B valuation. The Peer-Median method examined three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M, Series A), Fourth Power ($150M, Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M, Series A)—yielding a median of $150M with a range of $110M–$200M.

The two methods produced a wide triangulated range: a low of $128M, a median of $12.6B, and a high of $28.7B. This dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding whether the Stage-Progression method's assumption (anchored to total capital raised rather than operating metrics) appropriately values an early-stage climate-tech business versus the Peer-Median approach's reliance on a limited sample. Key unknowns include OpenStar's technical differentiation relative to the peer set, path to revenue, and capital efficiency relative to comparable stage and sector benchmarks.
